sourceMovie.title: Milk
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Milk for the same reason it broke through in 2008: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Gus Van Sant keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The history and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Kingdom of Heaven
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Milk clicked for you, Kingdom of Heaven (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in history and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Milk?
faq.answer: Try Kingdom of Heaven, The Trial of the Chicago 7, The Queen—each shares DNA with Milk while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Milk?
faq.answer: Milk (2008) is best described as History, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Milk worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this history lane.
